title: Learning Engineering: A Developing Discipline (Chapter 1)
topic_justification: Defines the field, traces origins, frames LE as distinct from instructional design

title: What is Learning Engineering? (Saxberg 2016)
topic_justification: Canonical public-facing LE manifesto; widely assigned; defines practitioner role

title: The Science of Learning: 77 Studies That Every Teacher Should Know
topic_justification: Applied synthesis of learning science; bridges theory and practice

title: Improving Students' Learning With Effective Practice Techniques
venue: Psychological Science in the Public Interest
topic_justification: Six high-utility learning techniques; spaced practice, retrieval, elaborative interrogation

title: Human Systems Integration Handbook
topic_justification: Defines HSI as engineering discipline; foundational for treating human as design constraint

title: Cognitive Load Theory
authors: Sweller J, van Merriënboer J, Paas F
topic_justification: The foundational LE theory for instructional design and adaptive systems

title: Learning Engineering Toolkit (LEVI)
topic_justification: Practitioner-facing LE process framework; evidence standards; iterative design cycle

title: Knowledge-Learning-Instruction Framework
topic_justification: KLI framework: maps knowledge types to learning processes to instruction principles

title: Educational Data Mining: A Review of the State-of-the-Art
topic_justification: Foundational EDM review; taxonomy of methods; applications survey

title: Learning Analytics: The Emergence of a Discipline
topic_justification: Defines learning analytics as field; situates in broader data science context

title: Competency-Based Education: A Framework for Practice
topic_justification: Competency frameworks as knowledge representation for workforce learning

title: Intelligent Tutoring Systems: A Comprehensive Review
topic_justification: Recent ITS review; architecture; effectiveness; AI integration trends

title: A Survey of Large Language Models for Education
topic_justification: Comprehensive survey of LLM applications in education; tutoring, feedback, assessment

title: Simulation-Based Learning: Theory and Practice
topic_justification: Meta-analysis of game/simulation learning; transfer; instructional design moderators

title: A Practical Guide to Cognitive Task Analysis
authors: Crandall B, Klein G, Hoffman R
topic_justification: Foundational CTA methods guide; elicitation techniques; applied expertise research

title: Evaluating Training Programs: The Four Levels
topic_justification: Kirkpatrick model; reaction/learning/behavior/results; universal training evaluation framework

title: Human Error
topic_justification: Foundational theory of human error in high-consequence systems; Swiss cheese model

title: Algorithmic Fairness in Education
topic_justification: Algorithmic bias in adaptive learning; fairness metrics; equity implications

title: Toward a Science of Exceptional Achievement
topic_justification: Deliberate practice; evidence standards for expertise; measurable performance

title: IEEE ICICLE Body of Knowledge — current version
topic_justification: Defines LE competency areas; vocabulary; practice standards; credentialing basis

title: Design-Based Research: An Emerging Paradigm for Educational Inquiry
topic_justification: Defines DBR methodology; iterative design + empirical research in authentic contexts

title: Design Experiments: Theoretical and Methodological Challenges in Creating Complex Interventions in Classroom Settings
topic_justification: Most-cited paper in JLS (2800+ citations). Establishes design-based research as the canonical methodology for testing LE interventions in authentic classrooms. Directly addresses gap g_008: T17 needs DBR academic anchors.
selection_source: Landscape foundational anchor — promoted from LS-SEED-001 (landscape_source=LE-LS-AP-003)
notes: Most-cited paper in JLS (2800+ citations). Establishes design-based research as the canonical methodology for testing LE interventions in authentic classrooms. Directly addresses gap g_008: T17 needs DBR academic anchors.

title: The State of Educational Data Mining in 2009: A Review and Future Visions
topic_justification: 1500+ citations. Codified EDM as a distinct discipline with its own methods and taxonomy. T04 currently has zero academic papers; this is the field-defining anchor. Distinct from Romero & Ventura 2010 (LE-T1-009) which is a general review —
selection_source: Landscape foundational anchor — promoted from LS-SEED-002 (landscape_source=LE-LS-AP-005)
notes: 1500+ citations. Codified EDM as a distinct discipline with its own methods and taxonomy. T04 currently has zero academic papers; this is the field-defining anchor. Distinct from Romero & Ventura 2010 (LE-T1-009) which is a general review — Baker & Yacef 2009 is the constitutive naming act for EDM.

title: Knowledge Tracing: Modeling the Acquisition of Procedural Knowledge
topic_justification: 2500+ citations. Formal presentation of Bayesian Knowledge Tracing (BKT) — the dominant probabilistic student model still deployed in production ITS worldwide. The algorithm behind all competency mastery tracking in modern adaptive learning
selection_source: Landscape foundational anchor — promoted from LS-SEED-003 (landscape_source=LE-LS-AP-011)
notes: 2500+ citations. Formal presentation of Bayesian Knowledge Tracing (BKT) — the dominant probabilistic student model still deployed in production ITS worldwide. The algorithm behind all competency mastery tracking in modern adaptive learning systems.

title: Cognitive Tutors: Lessons Learned
authors: Anderson JR, Corbett AT, Koedinger KR, Pelletier R
topic_justification: 1800+ citations. Decade synthesis of Cognitive Tutor development: ACT-R production rules, BKT, empirical learning gains in real schools. One of the most influential synthesis papers in ITS. Any one-hop from this will surface the surrounding
selection_source: Landscape foundational anchor — promoted from LS-SEED-004 (landscape_source=LE-LS-AP-014)
notes: 1800+ citations. Decade synthesis of Cognitive Tutor development: ACT-R production rules, BKT, empirical learning gains in real schools. One of the most influential synthesis papers in ITS. Any one-hop from this will surface the surrounding cognitive tutor and ITS literature.

title: Cognitive load during problem solving: Effects on learning
topic_justification: 4000+ citations. The founding paper of Cognitive Load Theory — most replicated finding in educational psychology and a standard LE design rule. T01 currently has zero academic papers; LE-T1-006 is the 2019 Sweller textbook; this 1988 paper
selection_source: Landscape foundational anchor — promoted from LS-SEED-005 (landscape_source=LE-LS-AP-008)
notes: 4000+ citations. The founding paper of Cognitive Load Theory — most replicated finding in educational psychology and a standard LE design rule. T01 currently has zero academic papers; LE-T1-006 is the 2019 Sweller textbook; this 1988 paper is the citation hub for the entire CLT empirical literature. One-hop will recover worked-examples, split-attention, and redundancy effect papers.

title: Abstract planning and perceptual chunks: Elements of expertise in geometry
topic_justification: 650+ citations. Foundational paper encoding domain expertise as production rules for ITS — the methodological basis for Cognitive Tutor. One-hop will surface the expertise modeling and ITS knowledge-component literature.
selection_source: Landscape foundational anchor — promoted from LS-SEED-006 (landscape_source=LE-LS-AP-001)
notes: 650+ citations. Foundational paper encoding domain expertise as production rules for ITS — the methodological basis for Cognitive Tutor. One-hop will surface the expertise modeling and ITS knowledge-component literature.

title: SOAR: An Architecture for General Intelligence
authors: Laird JE, Newell A, Rosenbloom PS
topic_justification: 5000+ citations. Established SOAR and the chunking mechanism — a computational model of procedural skill acquisition from practice. Provides T01 with its cognitive architecture anchor alongside CLT. One-hop from SOAR will surface cognitive
selection_source: Landscape foundational anchor — promoted from LS-SEED-007 (landscape_source=LE-LS-AP-015)
notes: 5000+ citations. Established SOAR and the chunking mechanism — a computational model of procedural skill acquisition from practice. Provides T01 with its cognitive architecture anchor alongside CLT. One-hop from SOAR will surface cognitive architecture, ACT-R, and problem-solving literature foundational to ITS design.

title: Intelligent tutoring systems
authors: Albert T. Corbett, Kenneth R. Koedinger, John R. Anderson
topic_justification: Documented the full architecture of Bayesian Knowledge Tracing and the Cognitive Tutor student model. The chapter that operationalized real-time student modeling and served as the blueprint for adaptive learning software for the next two de
notes: Documented the full architecture of Bayesian Knowledge Tracing and the Cognitive Tutor student model. The chapter that operationalized real-time student modeling and served as the blueprint for adaptive learning software for the next two decades.

title: Computer Support for Knowledge-Building Communities
topic_justification: Engineered the socio-cognitive framework for collaborative digital learning platforms. Established that effective online learning environments must support collective knowledge construction. Foundational for CSCL platform design, modern lea
notes: Engineered the socio-cognitive framework for collaborative digital learning platforms. Established that effective online learning environments must support collective knowledge construction. Foundational for CSCL platform design, modern learning management systems, and collaborative LE tools.

title: Immersive interfaces for engagement and learning
topic_justification: Established the engineering parameters for using immersive virtual environments in STEM education. Demonstrated that multi-user virtual environments and augmented reality can support complex cognition and inquiry skills not achievable throu
notes: Established the engineering parameters for using immersive virtual environments in STEM education. Demonstrated that multi-user virtual environments and augmented reality can support complex cognition and inquiry skills not achievable through conventional instruction. Positioned XR as a serious learning engineering domain.

title: Improving students' help-seeking skills using metacognitive feedback in an intelligent tutoring system
authors: Ido Roll, Vincent Aleven, Bruce M. McLaren, Kenneth R. Koedinger
topic_justification: Demonstrated that LE methods can be applied beyond domain knowledge to model and improve learner metacognition and self-regulation. Proved that intelligent tutors can engineer help-seeking behavior, not just subject-matter proficiency — bro
notes: Demonstrated that LE methods can be applied beyond domain knowledge to model and improve learner metacognition and self-regulation. Proved that intelligent tutors can engineer help-seeking behavior, not just subject-matter proficiency — broadening the scope of what LE can target.

title: Design principles for virtual humans in educational technology environments
authors: Scotty D. Craig, Nicolaus L. Schroeder
venue: International Journal of Artificial Intelligence in Education
topic_justification: Synthesized human-factors engineering principles for designing pedagogical agents. Showed that voice, appearance, and persona of virtual instructors systematically affect learner cognitive load and perceived credibility — establishing that
notes: Synthesized human-factors engineering principles for designing pedagogical agents. Showed that voice, appearance, and persona of virtual instructors systematically affect learner cognitive load and perceived credibility — establishing that LE must integrate psychological design alongside algorithmic design.

title: The voice quality of pedagogical agent impacts learning and agent perceptions
authors: Ryan F. Siegle, Scotty D. Craig
topic_justification: Empirically demonstrated that voice quality (prosody, naturalness, warmth) of a pedagogical agent significantly affects both learning outcomes and learner perception. Illustrates the meticulous human factors engineering required in modern A
notes: Empirically demonstrated that voice quality (prosody, naturalness, warmth) of a pedagogical agent significantly affects both learning outcomes and learner perception. Illustrates the meticulous human factors engineering required in modern AI-driven learning systems.

title: The doer effect at scale: Investigating correlation and causation across seven courses
authors: Rachel Van Campenhout, Bill Jerome, Benny G. Johnson
venue: Proceedings of LAK23: 13th International Learning Analytics and Knowledge Conference
topic_justification: Leveraged telemetry from large-scale online platforms to isolate the causal effect of active practice (doing) vs. passive reading on learning outcomes across seven courses. Provides large-sample empirical validation of one of learning engin
notes: Leveraged telemetry from large-scale online platforms to isolate the causal effect of active practice (doing) vs. passive reading on learning outcomes across seven courses. Provides large-sample empirical validation of one of learning engineering's core design principles at scale.

title: Generalizable Learning Engineering Adoption Maturity Model
authors: Shawn Blake-Plock, Scotty D. Craig, Emily Czerwinski, Jim Goodell, Jason Lis, Katherine McEldoon, Kristopher Owens, Julian Stodd, Steven Schatz
topic_justification: Defines a multi-level organizational capability model for assessing and advancing an institution's LE practice. Provides a structured framework for moving from ad-hoc instructional design toward systematic, data-driven learning engineering
notes: Defines a multi-level organizational capability model for assessing and advancing an institution's LE practice. Provides a structured framework for moving from ad-hoc instructional design toward systematic, data-driven learning engineering — the field's primary tool for organizational self-assessment.

title: The Design of Everyday Things
topic_justification: Universal LE design reference. Revised/expanded edition of 1988 classic.
notes: Universal LE design reference. Revised/expanded edition of 1988 classic.

title: An Introduction to Human Factors Engineering
authors: Wickens CD, Lee JD, Liu Y, Gordon-Becker S
topic_justification: The HFE textbook; cognitive workload meets system design.
notes: The HFE textbook; cognitive workload meets system design.

title: Cognitive Work Analysis: Toward Safe, Productive, and Healthy Computer-Based Work
topic_justification: Work domain analysis for complex systems; defense/healthcare LE.
notes: Work domain analysis for complex systems; defense/healthcare LE.

title: Safety-I and Safety-II: The Past and Future of Safety Management
topic_justification: Resilience engineering; complements Reason's Human Error.
notes: Resilience engineering; complements Reason's Human Error.

title: A Revision of Bloom's Taxonomy: An Overview
topic_justification: THE competency taxonomy in LE. 7400+ citations.
notes: THE competency taxonomy in LE. 7400+ citations.

title: Knowing What Students Know: The Science and Design of Educational Assessment
authors: Pellegrino JW, Chudowsky N, Glaser R
topic_justification: Evidence-centered design + knowledge representation. NRC landmark.
notes: Evidence-centered design + knowledge representation. NRC landmark.

title: A Brief Introduction to Evidence-Centered Design
authors: Mislevy RJ, Almond RG, Lukas JF
topic_justification: Competencies as evidence arguments; Behrens lineage.
notes: Competencies as evidence arguments; Behrens lineage.

title: The Theory Underlying Concept Maps and How to Construct Them
topic_justification: Concept mapping methodology for CTA and curriculum design.
notes: Concept mapping methodology for CTA and curriculum design.

title: Perspectives on Cognitive Task Analysis: Historical Origins and Modern Communities of Practice
topic_justification: Comprehensive CTA methods review.
notes: Comprehensive CTA methods review.

title: Varieties of Knowledge Elicitation Techniques
topic_justification: Canonical taxonomy of elicitation methods.
notes: Canonical taxonomy of elicitation methods.

title: Categorization and Representation of Physics Problems by Experts and Novices
authors: Chi MTH, Feltovich PJ, Glaser R
topic_justification: Foundational expertise cognition: how experts categorize problems by deep structure vs novices by surface features.
notes: Foundational expertise cognition: how experts categorize problems by deep structure vs novices by surface features.

title: Turning Research Into Results: A Guide to Selecting the Right Performance Solutions
topic_justification: Evidence-based CTA-to-curriculum pipeline.
notes: Evidence-based CTA-to-curriculum pipeline.

title: Sources of Power: How People Make Decisions
topic_justification: Naturalistic decision making; feeds CTA methodology.
notes: Naturalistic decision making; feeds CTA methodology.

title: Understanding by Design
topic_justification: Most-used curriculum framework in K-12 LE. Backward design.
notes: Most-used curriculum framework in K-12 LE. Backward design.

title: First Principles of Instruction
topic_justification: Distilled from all major ID theories. Saxberg cites.
notes: Distilled from all major ID theories. Saxberg cites.

title: Ten Steps to Complex Learning: A Systematic Approach to Four-Component Instructional Design
topic_justification: 4C/ID model; links CLT to curriculum design.
notes: 4C/ID model; links CLT to curriculum design.

title: The Systematic Design of Instruction
authors: Dick W, Carey L, Carey JO
topic_justification: The ADDIE textbook.
notes: The ADDIE textbook.

title: Instructional Theory and Technology for the New Paradigm of Education
topic_justification: Transition from ID to personalized/adaptive. Bridges ID→LE.
notes: Transition from ID to personalized/adaptive. Bridges ID→LE.

title: Handbook of Human Systems Integration
significance: The definitive reference for HSI methodology. Establishes the six HSI domains (manpower, personnel, training, human factors, safety, health) and how they constrain system design. Directly applicable to learning system architecture where the human is the top-level design constraint.

title: Engineering Psychology and Human Performance
authors: Christopher D. Wickens; Justin G. Hollands; Simon Banbury; Raja Parasuraman
significance: Standard textbook applying information-processing psychology to system design. Covers attention, perception, memory, and decision-making constraints that learning engineers must account for. The cognitive engineering perspective that bridges T01 (learning science) and T02 (systems engineering).

title: Joint Cognitive Systems: Foundations of Cognitive Systems Engineering
significance: Reframes human-machine systems as joint cognitive systems where performance emerges from the coupling of human and artifact. Directly relevant to adaptive learning systems where tutor and student form a joint cognitive unit.

title: Information Processing and Human-Machine Interaction: An Approach to Cognitive Engineering
significance: Introduced the skill-rule-knowledge (SRK) framework for modeling human performance at different levels of cognitive control. SRK is directly applicable to ITS design: different tutoring strategies are needed depending on whether the learner is operating at skill, rule, or knowledge level.

title: Sociotechnical Systems Theory as a Diagnostic Tool for Examining Underperformance in Integrated Learning Engineering Teams
authors: Steven J. Landry; Purdue Engineering Education
significance: Applies Emery and Trist's sociotechnical systems theory to diagnose breakdowns in integrated LE teams. One of the few papers explicitly connecting STS principles to learning engineering practice.

title: The Knowledge-Learning-Instruction Framework: Bridging the Science-Practice Chasm to Enhance Robust Student Learning
authors: Kenneth R. Koedinger; Albert T. Corbett; Charles Perfetti
significance: THE bridge paper between knowledge representation and learning engineering. Introduces the KLI taxonomy: knowledge components (facts, skills, principles) mapped to learning events (instruction, practice, assessment) mapped to instructional principles. The most cited framework for LE knowledge representation.

title: Learning Factors Analysis — A General Method for Cognitive Model Comparison on Student Performance Data
authors: Hao Cen; Kenneth R. Koedinger; Brian Junker
venue: International Conference on Intelligent Tutoring Systems (ITS 2006)
significance: Introduced Learning Factors Analysis (LFA), a data-driven method for discovering and validating knowledge components from student performance data. Enables automated refinement of the knowledge model underlying an ITS — the practical operationalization of T05 for adaptive systems.

title: New Potentials for Data-Driven Intelligent Tutoring System Development and Optimization
authors: Kenneth R. Koedinger; Elizabeth A. McLaughlin; John C. Stamper
significance: Demonstrates how data-driven knowledge component discovery can optimize ITS. The paper shows that well-specified knowledge components predict student performance better than poorly specified ones — empirical validation that knowledge representation quality drives learning outcomes.

title: Advances in Intelligent Tutoring Systems
authors: Roger Nkambou; Jacqueline Bourdeau; Riichiro Mizoguchi
significance: Comprehensive volume on ITS architecture with dedicated chapters on domain ontology engineering, learner model ontology, and instructional planning ontology. Mizoguchi's chapter on ontological engineering for ITS is the most systematic treatment of knowledge representation for adaptive learning systems.

title: Instructional Factors Analysis: A Cognitive Model for Multiple Instructional Interventions
authors: Min Chi; Kenneth R. Koedinger; Gareth J. Gordon; Pamela Jordan; Kurt VanLehn
venue: International Conference on Educational Data Mining (EDM 2011)
significance: Extended Learning Factors Analysis to model the interaction between knowledge components and instructional methods. Shows that the same knowledge component may require different instructional approaches depending on its type — linking knowledge representation directly to instructional design decisions.

title: Educational Knowledge Graph: A Survey and Empirical Study
authors: Yupei Zhang; Renyu Zhu; Ming Li
venue: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
significance: Comprehensive survey of knowledge graph construction and application in education. Covers concept extraction, prerequisite relationship mining, and knowledge graph-based recommendation. Maps the state of the art in computational knowledge representation for learning systems.

title: Working Minds: A Practitioner's Guide to Cognitive Task Analysis
authors: Beth Crandall; Gary Klein; Robert R. Hoffman
significance: The definitive practitioner's guide to CTA methods. Covers critical decision method, knowledge audit, simulation interviews, and concept mapping — the core elicitation toolkit for learning engineers building expert models. Used in military, healthcare, and aviation training design.

title: The Cambridge Handbook of Expertise and Expert Performance
authors: K. Anders Ericsson; Neil Charness; Paul J. Feltovich; Robert R. Hoffman
significance: Comprehensive handbook covering the science of expertise across domains. Establishes the theoretical foundation for why expert knowledge must be elicited (it's largely tacit and automatic) and what methods work. The scientific backbone for T09.

title: Cognitive Task Analysis
authors: Richard E. Clark; David F. Feldon; Jeroen J. G. van Merriënboer; Kenneth A. Yates; Sean Early
venue: Handbook of Research on Educational Communications and Technology (Springer)
significance: Authoritative chapter connecting CTA methods directly to instructional design. Shows how elicited expert knowledge maps to training objectives, practice sequences, and assessment criteria. The bridge paper between T09 (elicitation) and T03 (LE process) / T12 (instructional design).

title: Applied Cognitive Task Analysis (ACTA): A Practitioner's Toolkit for Understanding Cognitive Task Demands
authors: Laura G. Militello; Robert J. B. Hutton
significance: Introduced the streamlined ACTA methodology: task diagram, knowledge audit, simulation interview, and cognitive demands table. Designed to be usable by practitioners (not just researchers). Widely adopted in military and healthcare training design as a lightweight CTA alternative.

title: Protocol Analysis: Verbal Reports as Data
authors: K. Anders Ericsson; Herbert A. Simon
significance: The methodological foundation for think-aloud protocol analysis. Ericsson and Simon established the conditions under which verbal reports are valid data about cognitive processes. Every CTA method that uses think-aloud builds on this work.
